metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_4145
metadata.comment:
Rationale: "CA2AP plays a role in Synaptic Proteostasis"
Firstly, in this study, the authors carried out experiments with Cindr gene (fly homolog of the Alzheimer's risk gene CD2AP) and they observed that:

Cindr is required for synaptic maturation (high lethality in cindr homozygotes phenotypes). They demonstrated an incomplete boutons differentiation in mutant phenotypes of cindr (Figs 2D, 2F, S2D and S3B), less active zones (Fig S6E) and abnormal synaptic terminals elongation (Fig S6B and S6E); also less synaptic vesicle endocytosis. But mainly, they observed that the loss of cindr provokes increases of Synapsin and PMCA proteins (Figs 5A and 5B) and a reduction of 14-3-3zheta protein, all related to defects in proteasome function.

After they observed in (Figures 7A and 7B) elevations in the synaptic proteins Synapsin-1 and Synaptophysin, also of an increase of PMCA or reduction of 14-3-3 protein levels in brain homogenates prepared from 5-month-old Cd2ap null animals, like previously for Drosophila findings. Also, in Fig 7C they confirmed the reduction of ubiquitin-proteasome system activity in Cd2ap null mice, through in vitro proteasome assay.

In conclusion, they hypothesize that CD2AP/Cindr regulate/play a role in synaptic proteostasis.

20/01/2021 Dnyanada: The annotation uses both drosophila and mouse biological systems, CD2AP_MOUSE was used as the Uniprot identifier for this annotation.. Experimental description: Cd2apKO mouse used previously in PMID: 10514378
Mice lacking CD2AP were generated by replacing the exon encoding the first SRC homology 3 (SH3) domain of CD2AP with a neomycin resistance gene. Homozygous CD2AP knockout (KO) mice were generated from heterozygous mice with a normal frequency of 25%.
